markdown.markdown:
```{r setup, include=FALSE} library(tidyverse) library(lubridate) library(ISOweek) library(leaflet) library(zoo) library(forecast) library(spdep) library(sf) library(councildown) library(classInt) ``` ```{r} noise <- read.csv("../Data/311_Service_Requests.csv") # List all types and descriptors unique(noise$Problem..formerly.Complaint.Type) unique(noise$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) # Keep only non-social noise noise <- noise %>% filter(Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor. %in% c("Noise: Construction Before/After Hours (NM1)", "Noise: Alarms (NR3)", "Noise: Construction Equipment (NC1)", "Engine Idling", "Car/Truck Horn")) # List types and descriptors again unique(noise$Problem..formerly.Complaint.Type) unique(noise$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) noise <- noise %>% mutate(Created.Date = mdy_hms(Created.Date), Closed.Date = mdy_hms(Closed.Date), Resolution.Action.Updated.Date = mdy_hms(Resolution.Action.Updated.Date)) # Considering dropping 1.5% of observations: without lat/long, these are marginally more difficult to geocode. noise_ll <- noise %>% filter(!is.na(Latitude)) range(noise_ll$Created.Date) # Date range aligns with SONYC noise_ll <- noise_ll %>% select(Problem..formerly.Complaint.Type., Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor., Created.Date, Latitude, Longitude, Location) noise_ll$week <- strftime(noise_ll$Created.Date, format = "%V") %>% as.numeric() noise_ll$month <- format(noise_ll$Created.Date, "%Y-%m") noise_ll$month <- as.Date(paste0(noise_ll$month, "-01"), format = "%Y-%m-%d") noise_ll$year <- format(noise_ll$Created.Date, "%Y") noise_weekly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(year, week) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") noise_monthly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(month) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") noise_yearly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(year) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") ``` # Graph Complaints (Weekly/Monthly/Yearly) ```{r} ggplot(noise_weekly, aes(x = interaction(year, week), y = total_complaints, group = 1)) + geom_line() + geom_smooth(se = FALSE) + labs(title = "Weekly Street Noise Complaint Counts in NYC", x = "Week", y = "Number of complaints") + theme_minimal() monthly_complaints <- ggplot(noise_monthly, aes(x = month, y = total_complaints, group = 1)) + geom_line() + geom_smooth(se = FALSE) + scale_x_date(date_breaks = "4 months", date_labels = "%Y-%m") + labs(title = "Monthly Street Noise Complaint Counts in NYC", x = "Month", y = "Number of complaints") + theme_minimal() monthly_complaints # Complaints ebb and flow month-to-month, with highs between April and June and reliable October spikes. # Could be seasonal, check more later. saveRDS(monthly_complaints, "../Visuals/monthly_complaints.rds") ggplot(noise_yearly, aes(x = year, y = total_complaints, group = 1)) + geom_line() + labs(title = "Yearly Street Noise Complaint Counts in NYC", x = "Year", y = "Number of complaints") + theme_minimal() # Identify week-level anomalies (rolling z-score) noise_weekly <- noise_weekly %>% arrange(year, week) %>% mutate( roll_mean = rollmean(total_complaints, 8, fill = NA, align = "right"), roll_sd = rollapply(total_complaints, 8, sd, fill = NA, align = "right"), z = (total_complaints - roll_mean) / roll_sd, spike = z > 2, date = ISOweek2date(paste0(year, "-W", sprintf("%02d", week), "-1")) ) ggplot(noise_weekly, aes(x = date, y = total_complaints)) + geom_line() + geom_line(aes(y = roll_mean), linetype = "dashed") + geom_point(data = subset(noise_weekly, spike), color = "red") + labs(title = "Weekly Street Noise Complaint Counts with Spikes Highlighted", x = "Week", y = "Number of complaints") + theme_minimal() # No hotspots identified at the 4-week level, 1 at the 6-week level. # Using an 8-week average, 4 hotspots were identified, in April, June, and September. ``` # Monthly seasonality tests for all types ```{r} noise_monthly_type <- noise_ll %>% group_by(month, Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") count(noise_ll, Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) %>% mutate(`%` = round(n/sum(n) * 100)) %>% arrange(desc(n)) ggplot(noise_monthly_type, aes(x = month, y = total_complaints)) + geom_line() + facet_wrap(~ Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor., scales = "free_y") + labs(title = "Monthly Noise Complaints by Type", x = "Month", y = "Total Complaints") + theme_minimal() # 48% of non-social noise complaints regard Construction Before/After Hours. Total construction complaints account for 62%. Vehicle noise (horns and engines) account for 29%, and alarms account for 8%. # The construction pattern seems to be driving the overall nonsocial noise pattern. ``` # Monthly seasonality checks from SONYC (and 311 dashboard) ```{r} # Create a timeseries object noise_ts <- ts(noise_weekly$total_complaints, frequency = 52, start = c(2016,11)) # Automatically assign best ARIMA model using AIC fit_arima <- auto.arima(noise_ts, seasonal = TRUE) summary(fit_arima) # Best ARIMA model still incorporates seasonality without summer-seasonal social noise. # Separate trend from seasonality and check for a pattern. decomp <- stl(noise_ts, s.window = "periodic") plot(decomp) # Seasonal pattern exists, but is more complicated than "more complaints in the spring and fall". # Compare this to the models recommended by 311 dashboard for related complaints fit_naive <- naive(noise_ts) fit_tbats <- tbats(noise_ts) # All models' residuals pass Ljung-Box test: no pattern suggesting the model is missing something systematically. checkresiduals(fit_naive) checkresiduals(fit_arima) checkresiduals(fit_tbats) # Cross-validate models and compare MSE # e_arima <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { # forecast(auto.arima(y), h = h) # }) # # e_naive <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { # forecast(naive(y), h = h) # }) # # e_tbats <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { # forecast(tbats(y), h = h) # }) # # sprintf("ARIMA MSE: %s", format(mean(e_arima^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) # sprintf("Naive MSE: %s", format(mean(e_naive^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) # sprintf("TBATS MSE: %s", format(mean(e_tbats^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) # Naive model is the best predictor in terms of MSE. ``` # Map Noise Complaints by NTA ```{r} noise_sf <- st_as_sf(noise_ll, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326) # Read in demographics data from councilcount, replace likely population flukes with 0. ntas <- st_read("../Data/nta-demo.geojson") %>% st_transform(st_crs(noise_sf)) %>% select(nta, total_popE, geometry) %>% mutate(total_popE = if_else(total_popE < 100, 0, total_popE)) noise_with_nta <- st_join(noise_sf, ntas, join = st_within) nta_counts <- noise_with_nta %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% group_by(nta) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") nta_map <- ntas %>% left_join(nta_counts, by = "nta") %>% mutate( total_complaints = coalesce(total_complaints, 0), complaints_pc = case_when( total_complaints == 0 ~ 0, total_popE == 0 ~ NA_real_, TRUE ~ (total_complaints / total_popE) * 1000)) jenks_breaks <- classIntervals(nta_map$total_complaints[nta_map$total_complaints > 0], n = 5, style = "jenks")$brks # Adjust around some big jumps in the distribution jenks_breaks[3] = 1720 jenks_breaks[5] = 6452 warm_pal_jenks <- leaflet::colorBin(palette = pal_nycc("warm"), domain = nta_map$total_complaints, bins = jenks_breaks, na.color = "transparent") nta_map$fill <- ifelse(nta_map$total_complaints == 0, "white", warm_pal_jenks(nta_map$total_complaints)) # Map raw complaints leaflet(nta_map) %>% addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% addPolygons( fillColor = ~fill, fillOpacity = 0.7, color = "white", weight = 1, popup = ~paste0( "", nta, "
", "Complaints: ", format(total_complaints, big.mark = ",") ) ) %>% addLegend( "bottomright", pal = warm_pal_jenks, values = ~total_complaints, title = "Total Complaints" ) # Hillcrest is even more of an outlier than it was among all 311 complaints. Quick google suggests this could be due to alarm complaints. # Chelsea, FiDi, UWS, Forest Hills, Williamsburg, and Fort Greene may also have hotspot potential. ``` # Quick aside - break down complaints by type in Hillcrest ```{r} # Investigate alarms alarms <- noise_with_nta %>% filter(Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor. == "Noise: Alarms (NR3)") alarm_counts <- alarms %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% group_by(nta) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") alarm_map <- ntas %>% left_join(alarm_counts, by = "nta") %>% mutate( total_complaints = coalesce(total_complaints, 0), complaints_pc = case_when( total_complaints == 0 ~ 0, total_popE == 0 ~ NA_real_, TRUE ~ (total_complaints / total_popE) * 1000)) alarm_breaks <- classIntervals(alarm_map$total_complaints[alarm_map$total_complaints > 0], n = 5, style = "jenks")$brks warm_pal_alarm <- leaflet::colorBin(palette = pal_nycc("warm"), domain = alarm_map$total_complaints, bins = alarm_breaks, na.color = "transparent") alarm_map$fill <- ifelse(alarm_map$total_complaints == 0, "white", warm_pal_alarm(alarm_map$total_complaints)) # Map raw complaints leaflet(alarm_map) %>% addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% addPolygons( fillColor = ~fill, fillOpacity = 0.7, color = "white", weight = 1, popup = ~paste0( "", nta, "
", "Complaints: ", format(total_complaints, big.mark = ",") ) ) %>% addLegend( "bottomright", pal = warm_pal_alarm, values = ~total_complaints, title = "Total Complaints" ) # Alarms account for 33% of complaints in Hillcrest and 8% citywide. # Hillcrest is responsible for 10% of citywide complaints, but 43% of alarm complaints. # This explains part of the story, but most of Hillcrest's complaints are not alarm-related. type_counts <- noise_with_nta %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% group_by(nta, Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") hillcrest_counts <- type_counts %>% filter(nta == "Pomonok-Electchester-Hillcrest") hillcrest_counts # Horn complaints: 16% citywide, 59% in Hillcrest. Hillcrest accounts for 38% of citywide horn complaints. # Idling: 13% citywide, 7% Hillcrest, accounts for 5% citywide. # Construction: 48% citywide, <1% Hillcrest. # Equipment: 14% citywide, <1% Hillcrest. # It's a mix of alarms and horns, with the most common complaints citywide (construction) rarely occurring here. Later results show major hotspots at Union Turnpike between Parsons Blvd and 164th St. It's a 6-lane stroad near a lot of residential buildings, so maybe tenants are complaining about traffic? # While we're here, let's check the complaint breakdowns of other top NTAs. type_counts %>% filter(nta == "Financial District-Battery Park City") type_counts %>% filter(nta == "Tribeca-Civic Center") # Slightly more horns type_counts %>% filter(nta == "Murray Hill-Kips Bay") type_counts %>% filter(nta == "Chelsea-Hudson Yards") # Especially construction-heavy type_counts %>% filter(nta == "Midtown-Times Square") # More construction, fewer horns. # And investigate the biggest complaint clusters (>1,000 at same lat/long) cluster1 <- noise %>% filter(Location == "POINT (-73.803829510188 40.718715950412)") unique(cluster1$Incident.Address) # Queens Hospital Center, opposite a residential block sort(table(cluster1$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.)) # Overwhelmingly Car/Truck Horn, fair share of Alarms cluster2 <- noise %>% filter(Location == "POINT (-73.807760613387 40.720328269591)") unique(cluster2$Incident.Address) # Fire Station. FDNY Engine 315 & Ladder 125 sort(table(cluster2$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.)) # Even split between alarms and horns. cluster3 <- noise %>% filter(Location == "POINT (-73.809696824262 40.721959131993)") unique(cluster3$Incident.Address) # Synagogue and grade school. sort(table(cluster3$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.)) # Mainly alarms and horns, with more idling complaints than before. Likely pickup/dropoff traffic? cluster4 <- noise %>% filter(Location == "POINT (-73.809526041818 40.722387032895)") unique(cluster4$Incident.Address) # Seems to be a private residence. It's around the block from the synagogue/school. sort(table(cluster4$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.)) # Almost exclusively reporting car truck/horn. sort(table(cluster4$Resolution.Description)) # Police generally come but don't do anything. cluster5 <- noise %>% filter(Location == "POINT (-73.80791292173 40.722557273133)") unique(cluster5$Intersection.Street.1) unique(cluster5$Intersection.Street.2) # No address, just 160th St & 78th Ave in Queens. Totally residential intersection, but 1 block from the synagogue/school seen earlier. Likely backup from pickup/dropoff. sort(table(cluster5$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.)) # Mainly horns and idling, some alarms. Idling supports the school traffic hypothesis. sort(table(cluster5$Resolution.Description)) # 1 summons, 25 cases of "taking action" out of 1k complaints. # All of these clusters are within a ~3/4 mile radius, spanning Hillcrest and Pomonok. ``` # Complaints per capita calculations ```{r} # Map complaints per capita jenks_breaks_pc <- classIntervals( round(nta_map$complaints_pc[nta_map$total_popE > 0]), n = 5, style = "jenks")$brks # Include top NTA by complaints pc jenks_breaks_pc[5] = 221 warm_pal_jenks_pc <- leaflet::colorBin( palette = pal_nycc("warm"), domain = round(nta_map$complaints_pc), bins = jenks_breaks_pc, na.color = "grey" ) nta_map$fill_pc <- case_when( nta_map$total_popE == 0 ~ "grey", # no population → gray TRUE ~ warm_pal_jenks_pc(round(nta_map$complaints_pc)) ) # Hillcrest remains dominant after controlling for population. # Midtown, Tribeca, and maybe DUMBO appear brighter than their neighbors. ``` # Gi* Calculations ```{r} # Collapse complaints with duplicate locations for cluster analysis noise_collapsed <- noise_sf %>% group_by(Location) %>% summarise(total_obs = n(), .groups = "drop") # Project for distance-based neighbors (meters) noise_collapsed_proj <- st_transform(noise_collapsed, 32618) # UTM zone 18N for NYC coords <- st_coordinates(noise_collapsed_proj) # Define neighbors within 500 m neighbors <- dnearneigh(coords, 0, 500) weights <- nb2listw(neighbors, style = "W", zero.policy = TRUE) gi_star <- localG(noise_collapsed_proj$total_obs, listw = weights, zero.policy = TRUE) noise_collapsed_proj$GiZ <- as.numeric(gi_star) noise_collapsed_leaflet <- st_transform(noise_collapsed_proj, 4326) # back to WGS84 noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers <- noise_collapsed_leaflet %>% filter(GiZ > 2.58) # Narrows down to 783 coordinate pairs identified as hotspots in a 500m radius (significant at 1% level) # This is much fewer than we had when including social noise jenks_breaks_gi <- classIntervals( round(noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers$GiZ, 2), n = 3, style = "jenks")$brks # Tweaks jenks_breaks_gi[4] = 65.72 jenks_breaks_gi[3] = 46.61 jenks_breaks_gi[2] = 17.42 # The same Queens NTA remains the biggest outlier and clusters are also seen in Lower and Midtown Manhattan. # Hudson Square, Financial District, and Murray Hill are hotspots for non-social noise complaints. There's a bit of a yuppie connection between these areas, but they're also close to congested tunnels and bridges. # Look for additional hotspots with 5% significance... noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers_2 <- noise_collapsed_leaflet %>% filter(GiZ > 1.96) # 1,968 coordinate pairs identified as hotspots in a 500m radius (significant at 5% level) # Use similar breaks/palette since we just added a bunch of observations on the lower end jenks_breaks_gi[1] = 1.96 pal <- leaflet::colorBin( palette = pal_nycc("cool"), domain = round(noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers$GiZ, 2), bins = jenks_breaks_gi, na.color = "grey" ) # A pattern is emerging: aside from Hillcrest, the greatest non-social noise hotspots are in Midtown Manhattan and near Manhattan bridges/tunnels/highways: Lincoln Tunnel, Queens-Midtown Tunnel, Holland Tunnel, and Brooklyn Bridge/FDR Drive. ``` # Main visual ```{r} main_viz <- leaflet() %>% # Basemap addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # NTA CHOROPLETH (BASE LAYER) # ------------------------------------------------- addPolygons( data = nta_map, group = "NTA Complaints per Capita", fillColor = ~fill_pc, fillOpacity = 0.7, color = "white", weight = 1, popup = ~paste0( "", nta, "
", "Complaints per 1k Residents: ", round(complaints_pc) ) ) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # GI* HOTSPOT POINTS (OVERLAY) # ------------------------------------------------- addCircleMarkers( data = noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers_2, group = "Complaint Hotspots", radius = 5, fillColor = ~pal(GiZ), fillOpacity = 0.8, stroke = TRUE, weight = 1, color = "blue", popup = ~paste0( "Gi* Z-score: ", round(GiZ, 2), "
", "Total Complaints: ", total_obs ) ) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # LEGENDS # ------------------------------------------------- addLegend( position = "bottomright", pal = warm_pal_jenks_pc, values = nta_map$complaints_pc, title = "Complaints per 1,000 Residents", opacity = 0.7 ) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # LAYER CONTROL # ------------------------------------------------- addLayersControl( overlayGroups = c("Complaint Hotspots"), options = layersControlOptions(collapsed = FALSE) ) %>% # Default view setView(lng = -74.0, lat = 40.7, zoom = 10) main_viz saveRDS(main_viz, "../Visuals/main_viz.rds") ```

```{r setup, include=FALSE} library(tidyverse) library(lubridate) library(ISOweek) library(leaflet) library(zoo) library(forecast) library(spdep) library(sf) library(councildown) library(classInt) ``` # Load and clean data ```{r} noise <- read.csv("../Data/311_Service_Requests.csv") # List all types and descriptors unique(noise$Problem..formerly.Complaint.Type) unique(noise$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) # Keep only non-social noise noise <- noise %>% filter(Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor. %in% c("Noise: Construction Before/After Hours (NM1)", "Noise: Alarms (NR3)", "Noise: Construction Equipment (NC1)", "Engine Idling", "Car/Truck Horn")) # List types and descriptors again unique(noise$Problem..formerly.Complaint.Type) unique(noise$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) noise <- noise %>% mutate(Created.Date = mdy_hms(Created.Date), Closed.Date = mdy_hms(Closed.Date), Resolution.Action.Updated.Date = mdy_hms(Resolution.Action.Updated.Date)) # Considering dropping 1.5% of observations: without lat/long, these are marginally more difficult to geocode. noise_ll <- noise %>% filter(!is.na(Latitude)) range(noise_ll$Created.Date) # Date range aligns with SONYC noise_ll <- noise_ll %>% select(Problem..formerly.Complaint.Type., Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor., Created.Date, Latitude, Longitude, Location) noise_ll$week <- strftime(noise_ll$Created.Date, format = "%V") %>% as.numeric() noise_ll$month <- format(noise_ll$Created.Date, "%Y-%m") noise_ll$month <- as.Date(paste0(noise_ll$month, "-01"), format = "%Y-%m-%d") noise_ll$year <- format(noise_ll$Created.Date, "%Y") noise_weekly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(year, week) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") noise_monthly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(month) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") noise_yearly <- noise_ll %>% group_by(year) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") ``` # Graph Complaints (Weekly/Monthly/Yearly) ```{r} ggplot(noise_weekly, aes(x = interaction(year, week), y = total_complaints, group = 1)) + geom_line() + geom_smooth(se = FALSE) + labs(title = "Weekly Street Noise Complaint Counts in NYC", x = "Week", y = "Number of complaints") + theme_minimal() monthly_complaints <- ggplot(noise_monthly, aes(x = month, y = total_complaints, group = 1)) + geom_line() + geom_smooth(se = FALSE) + scale_x_date(date_breaks = "4 months", date_labels = "%Y-%m") + labs(title = "Monthly Street Noise Complaint Counts in NYC", x = "Month", y = "Number of complaints") + theme_minimal() monthly_complaints # Complaints ebb and flow month-to-month, with highs between April and June and reliable October spikes. # Could be seasonal, check more later. saveRDS(monthly_complaints, "../Visuals/monthly_complaints.rds") ggplot(noise_yearly, aes(x = year, y = total_complaints, group = 1)) + geom_line() + labs(title = "Yearly Street Noise Complaint Counts in NYC", x = "Year", y = "Number of complaints") + theme_minimal() # Identify week-level anomalies (rolling z-score) noise_weekly <- noise_weekly %>% arrange(year, week) %>% mutate( roll_mean = rollmean(total_complaints, 8, fill = NA, align = "right"), roll_sd = rollapply(total_complaints, 8, sd, fill = NA, align = "right"), z = (total_complaints - roll_mean) / roll_sd, spike = z > 2, date = ISOweek2date(paste0(year, "-W", sprintf("%02d", week), "-1")) ) ggplot(noise_weekly, aes(x = date, y = total_complaints)) + geom_line() + geom_line(aes(y = roll_mean), linetype = "dashed") + geom_point(data = subset(noise_weekly, spike), color = "red") + labs(title = "Weekly Street Noise Complaint Counts with Spikes Highlighted", x = "Week", y = "Number of complaints") + theme_minimal() # No hotspots identified at the 4-week level, 1 at the 6-week level. # Using an 8-week average, 4 hotspots were identified, in April, June, and September. ``` # Monthly seasonality tests for all types ```{r} noise_monthly_type <- noise_ll %>% group_by(month, Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") count(noise_ll, Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) %>% mutate(`%` = round(n/sum(n) * 100)) %>% arrange(desc(n)) ggplot(noise_monthly_type, aes(x = month, y = total_complaints)) + geom_line() + facet_wrap(~ Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor., scales = "free_y") + labs(title = "Monthly Noise Complaints by Type", x = "Month", y = "Total Complaints") + theme_minimal() # 48% of non-social noise complaints regard Construction Before/After Hours. Total construction complaints account for 62%. Vehicle noise (horns and engines) account for 29%, and alarms account for 8%. # The construction pattern seems to be driving the overall nonsocial noise pattern. ``` # Monthly seasonality checks from SONYC (and 311 dashboard) ```{r} # Create a timeseries object noise_ts <- ts(noise_weekly$total_complaints, frequency = 52, start = c(2016,11)) # Automatically assign best ARIMA model using AIC fit_arima <- auto.arima(noise_ts, seasonal = TRUE) summary(fit_arima) # Best ARIMA model still incorporates seasonality without summer-seasonal social noise. # Separate trend from seasonality and check for a pattern. decomp <- stl(noise_ts, s.window = "periodic") plot(decomp) # Seasonal pattern exists, but is more complicated than "more complaints in the spring and fall". # Compare this to the models recommended by 311 dashboard for related complaints fit_naive <- naive(noise_ts) fit_tbats <- tbats(noise_ts) # All models' residuals pass Ljung-Box test: no pattern suggesting the model is missing something systematically. checkresiduals(fit_naive) checkresiduals(fit_arima) checkresiduals(fit_tbats) # Cross-validate models and compare MSE # e_arima <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { # forecast(auto.arima(y), h = h) # }) # # e_naive <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { # forecast(naive(y), h = h) # }) # # e_tbats <- tsCV(noise_ts, forecastfunction = function(y, h) { # forecast(tbats(y), h = h) # }) # # sprintf("ARIMA MSE: %s", format(mean(e_arima^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) # sprintf("Naive MSE: %s", format(mean(e_naive^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) # sprintf("TBATS MSE: %s", format(mean(e_tbats^2, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ",")) # Naive model is the best predictor in terms of MSE. ``` # Map Noise Complaints by NTA ```{r} noise_sf <- st_as_sf(noise_ll, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326) # Read in demographics data from councilcount, replace likely population flukes with 0. ntas <- st_read("../Data/nta-demo.geojson") %>% st_transform(st_crs(noise_sf)) %>% select(nta, total_popE, geometry) %>% mutate(total_popE = if_else(total_popE < 100, 0, total_popE)) noise_with_nta <- st_join(noise_sf, ntas, join = st_within) nta_counts <- noise_with_nta %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% group_by(nta) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") nta_map <- ntas %>% left_join(nta_counts, by = "nta") %>% mutate( total_complaints = coalesce(total_complaints, 0), complaints_pc = case_when( total_complaints == 0 ~ 0, total_popE == 0 ~ NA_real_, TRUE ~ (total_complaints / total_popE) * 1000)) jenks_breaks <- classIntervals(nta_map$total_complaints[nta_map$total_complaints > 0], n = 5, style = "jenks")$brks # Adjust around some big jumps in the distribution jenks_breaks[3] = 1720 jenks_breaks[5] = 6452 warm_pal_jenks <- leaflet::colorBin(palette = pal_nycc("warm"), domain = nta_map$total_complaints, bins = jenks_breaks, na.color = "transparent") nta_map$fill <- ifelse(nta_map$total_complaints == 0, "white", warm_pal_jenks(nta_map$total_complaints)) # Map raw complaints leaflet(nta_map) %>% addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% addPolygons( fillColor = ~fill, fillOpacity = 0.7, color = "white", weight = 1, popup = ~paste0( "", nta, "
", "Complaints: ", format(total_complaints, big.mark = ",") ) ) %>% addLegend( "bottomright", pal = warm_pal_jenks, values = ~total_complaints, title = "Total Complaints" ) # Hillcrest is even more of an outlier than it was among all 311 complaints. Quick google suggests this could be due to alarm complaints. # Chelsea, FiDi, UWS, Forest Hills, Williamsburg, and Fort Greene may also have hotspot potential. ``` # Quick aside - break down complaints by type in Hillcrest ```{r} # Investigate alarms alarms <- noise_with_nta %>% filter(Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor. == "Noise: Alarms (NR3)") alarm_counts <- alarms %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% group_by(nta) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") alarm_map <- ntas %>% left_join(alarm_counts, by = "nta") %>% mutate( total_complaints = coalesce(total_complaints, 0), complaints_pc = case_when( total_complaints == 0 ~ 0, total_popE == 0 ~ NA_real_, TRUE ~ (total_complaints / total_popE) * 1000)) alarm_breaks <- classIntervals(alarm_map$total_complaints[alarm_map$total_complaints > 0], n = 5, style = "jenks")$brks warm_pal_alarm <- leaflet::colorBin(palette = pal_nycc("warm"), domain = alarm_map$total_complaints, bins = alarm_breaks, na.color = "transparent") alarm_map$fill <- ifelse(alarm_map$total_complaints == 0, "white", warm_pal_alarm(alarm_map$total_complaints)) # Map raw complaints leaflet(alarm_map) %>% addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% addPolygons( fillColor = ~fill, fillOpacity = 0.7, color = "white", weight = 1, popup = ~paste0( "", nta, "
", "Complaints: ", format(total_complaints, big.mark = ",") ) ) %>% addLegend( "bottomright", pal = warm_pal_alarm, values = ~total_complaints, title = "Total Complaints" ) # Alarms account for 33% of complaints in Hillcrest and 8% citywide. # Hillcrest is responsible for 10% of citywide complaints, but 43% of alarm complaints. # This explains part of the story, but most of Hillcrest's complaints are not alarm-related. type_counts <- noise_with_nta %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% group_by(nta, Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.) %>% summarise(total_complaints = n(), .groups = "drop") hillcrest_counts <- type_counts %>% filter(nta == "Pomonok-Electchester-Hillcrest") hillcrest_counts # Horn complaints: 16% citywide, 59% in Hillcrest. Hillcrest accounts for 38% of citywide horn complaints. # Idling: 13% citywide, 7% Hillcrest, accounts for 5% citywide. # Construction: 48% citywide, <1% Hillcrest. # Equipment: 14% citywide, <1% Hillcrest. # It's a mix of alarms and horns, with the most common complaints citywide (construction) rarely occurring here. Later results show major hotspots at Union Turnpike between Parsons Blvd and 164th St. It's a 6-lane stroad near a lot of residential buildings, so maybe tenants are complaining about traffic? # While we're here, let's check the complaint breakdowns of other top NTAs. type_counts %>% filter(nta == "Financial District-Battery Park City") type_counts %>% filter(nta == "Tribeca-Civic Center") # Slightly more horns type_counts %>% filter(nta == "Murray Hill-Kips Bay") type_counts %>% filter(nta == "Chelsea-Hudson Yards") # Especially construction-heavy type_counts %>% filter(nta == "Midtown-Times Square") # More construction, fewer horns. # And investigate the biggest complaint clusters (>1,000 at same lat/long) cluster1 <- noise %>% filter(Location == "POINT (-73.803829510188 40.718715950412)") unique(cluster1$Incident.Address) # Queens Hospital Center, opposite a residential block sort(table(cluster1$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.)) # Overwhelmingly Car/Truck Horn, fair share of Alarms cluster2 <- noise %>% filter(Location == "POINT (-73.807760613387 40.720328269591)") unique(cluster2$Incident.Address) # Fire Station. FDNY Engine 315 & Ladder 125 sort(table(cluster2$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.)) # Even split between alarms and horns. cluster3 <- noise %>% filter(Location == "POINT (-73.809696824262 40.721959131993)") unique(cluster3$Incident.Address) # Synagogue and grade school. sort(table(cluster3$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.)) # Mainly alarms and horns, with more idling complaints than before. Likely pickup/dropoff traffic? cluster4 <- noise %>% filter(Location == "POINT (-73.809526041818 40.722387032895)") unique(cluster4$Incident.Address) # Seems to be a private residence. It's around the block from the synagogue/school. sort(table(cluster4$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.)) # Almost exclusively reporting car truck/horn. sort(table(cluster4$Resolution.Description)) # Police generally come but don't do anything. cluster5 <- noise %>% filter(Location == "POINT (-73.80791292173 40.722557273133)") unique(cluster5$Intersection.Street.1) unique(cluster5$Intersection.Street.2) # No address, just 160th St & 78th Ave in Queens. Totally residential intersection, but 1 block from the synagogue/school seen earlier. Likely backup from pickup/dropoff. sort(table(cluster5$Problem.Detail..formerly.Descriptor.)) # Mainly horns and idling, some alarms. Idling supports the school traffic hypothesis. sort(table(cluster5$Resolution.Description)) # 1 summons, 25 cases of "taking action" out of 1k complaints. # All of these clusters are within a ~3/4 mile radius, spanning Hillcrest and Pomonok. ``` # Complaints per capita calculations ```{r} # Map complaints per capita jenks_breaks_pc <- classIntervals( round(nta_map$complaints_pc[nta_map$total_popE > 0]), n = 5, style = "jenks")$brks # Include top NTA by complaints pc jenks_breaks_pc[5] = 221 warm_pal_jenks_pc <- leaflet::colorBin( palette = pal_nycc("warm"), domain = round(nta_map$complaints_pc), bins = jenks_breaks_pc, na.color = "grey" ) nta_map$fill_pc <- case_when( nta_map$total_popE == 0 ~ "grey", # no population → gray TRUE ~ warm_pal_jenks_pc(round(nta_map$complaints_pc)) ) # Hillcrest remains dominant after controlling for population. # Midtown, Tribeca, and maybe DUMBO appear brighter than their neighbors. ``` # Gi* Calculations ```{r} # Collapse complaints with duplicate locations for cluster analysis noise_collapsed <- noise_sf %>% group_by(Location) %>% summarise(total_obs = n(), .groups = "drop") # Project for distance-based neighbors (meters) noise_collapsed_proj <- st_transform(noise_collapsed, 32618) # UTM zone 18N for NYC coords <- st_coordinates(noise_collapsed_proj) # Define neighbors within 500 m neighbors <- dnearneigh(coords, 0, 500) weights <- nb2listw(neighbors, style = "W", zero.policy = TRUE) gi_star <- localG(noise_collapsed_proj$total_obs, listw = weights, zero.policy = TRUE) noise_collapsed_proj$GiZ <- as.numeric(gi_star) noise_collapsed_leaflet <- st_transform(noise_collapsed_proj, 4326) # back to WGS84 noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers <- noise_collapsed_leaflet %>% filter(GiZ > 2.58) # Narrows down to 783 coordinate pairs identified as hotspots in a 500m radius (significant at 1% level) # This is much fewer than we had when including social noise jenks_breaks_gi <- classIntervals( round(noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers$GiZ, 2), n = 3, style = "jenks")$brks # Tweaks jenks_breaks_gi[4] = 65.72 jenks_breaks_gi[3] = 46.61 jenks_breaks_gi[2] = 17.42 # The same Queens NTA remains the biggest outlier and clusters are also seen in Lower and Midtown Manhattan. # Hudson Square, Financial District, and Murray Hill are hotspots for non-social noise complaints. There's a bit of a yuppie connection between these areas, but they're also close to congested tunnels and bridges. # Look for additional hotspots with 5% significance... noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers_2 <- noise_collapsed_leaflet %>% filter(GiZ > 1.96) # 1,968 coordinate pairs identified as hotspots in a 500m radius (significant at 5% level) # Use similar breaks/palette since we just added a bunch of observations on the lower end jenks_breaks_gi[1] = 1.96 pal <- leaflet::colorBin( palette = pal_nycc("cool"), domain = round(noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers$GiZ, 2), bins = jenks_breaks_gi, na.color = "grey" ) # A pattern is emerging: aside from Hillcrest, the greatest non-social noise hotspots are in Midtown Manhattan and near Manhattan bridges/tunnels/highways: Lincoln Tunnel, Queens-Midtown Tunnel, Holland Tunnel, and Brooklyn Bridge/FDR Drive. ``` # Main visual ```{r} main_viz <- leaflet() %>% # Basemap addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # NTA CHOROPLETH (BASE LAYER) # ------------------------------------------------- addPolygons( data = nta_map, group = "NTA Complaints per Capita", fillColor = ~fill_pc, fillOpacity = 0.7, color = "white", weight = 1, popup = ~paste0( "", nta, "
", "Complaints per 1k Residents: ", round(complaints_pc) ) ) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # GI* HOTSPOT POINTS (OVERLAY) # ------------------------------------------------- addCircleMarkers( data = noise_collapsed_leaflet_outliers_2, group = "Complaint Hotspots", radius = 5, fillColor = ~pal(GiZ), fillOpacity = 0.8, stroke = TRUE, weight = 1, color = "blue", popup = ~paste0( "Gi* Z-score: ", round(GiZ, 2), "
", "Total Complaints: ", total_obs ) ) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # LEGENDS # ------------------------------------------------- addLegend( position = "bottomright", pal = warm_pal_jenks_pc, values = nta_map$complaints_pc, title = "Complaints per 1,000 Residents", opacity = 0.7 ) %>% # ------------------------------------------------- # LAYER CONTROL # ------------------------------------------------- addLayersControl( overlayGroups = c("Complaint Hotspots"), options = layersControlOptions(collapsed = FALSE) ) %>% # Default view setView(lng = -74.0, lat = 40.7, zoom = 10) main_viz saveRDS(main_viz, "../Visuals/main_viz.rds") ```
